sn.e: Roberto seems upset with me today.

sn.s: "Dijiste que ibas a venir ayer y no llegaste."
sn.e: "You said you'd come yesterday and you didn't show up."
"I think there's a misunderstanding," I say.

sn.s: "Yo entendí que era hoy, no ayer."
sn.e: "I understood it was today, not yesterday."

sn.s: "Ah, entonces me equivoqué yo," dice él.
sn.e: "Ah, then I was the one who was wrong," he says.

sn.s: "No, tal vez no me expliqué bien."
sn.e: "No, maybe I didn't explain myself well."

sn.s: "Tranquilo, fue culpa de los dos," dice riendo.
sn.e: "Relax, it was both our faults," he says, laughing.

sn.s: "Quería aclararlo para que no quedáramos mal."
sn.e: "I wanted to clear it up so we wouldn't be on bad terms."

sn.s: Aclarar un malentendido salva una amistad.
sn.e: Clearing up a misunderstanding saves a friendship.

sn.s: Pedir perdón no es perder; es cuidar el vínculo.
sn.e: Apologizing isn't losing; it's protecting the bond.

sn.s: Hablar las cosas evita que crezcan.
sn.e: Talking things out keeps them from growing.
